tracks.title: Paper Lanterns
tracks.lore: Lana Del Delay taped crackling lantern footage from a Kyoto alley and built the pad around that loop — then ran it through delay until the lanterns echoed tomorrow.

tracks.title: Soft Static
tracks.lore: Recorded on a dying cassette deck; Lana Del Delay says the hiss is a feature — 'static is just delay that gave up on arriving.'

tracks.title: Morning Steam
tracks.lore: Written while waiting for kettle steam to clear the window — the first chord lands three seconds late on purpose.

tracks.title: Quiet Desk
tracks.lore: Jay-Zzz mixed this at whisper volume — liner notes say 'if you remember this track, you weren't the target audience.'

tracks.title: Tide Pool
tracks.lore: Field recordings from a tide pool at dawn; Lana Del Delay delayed each bubble until the tide came back twice.

tracks.title: Glass Garden
tracks.lore: Mallet hits sampled from wind chimes on a porch — still the 'uptempo' track on Born to Delay, which tells you everything.

tracks.title: Neon Commute
tracks.lore: Justin Tiger mapped an automated metro line into a chord progression — each stop syncs, every transfer hits like a chorus you can't unstick.

tracks.title: City Pulse
tracks.lore: Built from a kick recorded in a parking garage; Kayan East sampled the College Dropout bear mascot energy into the sub — the reverb tail answers from the eastbound lane.

tracks.title: Run Lights
tracks.lore: Composed for a friend training for a night marathon — Kayan East calls mile twelve 'a cultural moment' and timed the drop to a Graduation-pastel sunrise over the east river.

tracks.title: Open Road
tracks.lore: Kayan East imagined headlights on an empty highway where every sign points EAST — the filter sweep is the first curve past the neon district.

tracks.title: Skyline Drive
tracks.lore: Taylor Drift wrote this after her first clean hairpin in the matte white coupe — every arp is a downshift, every filter sweep is tire smoke catching the 1989-blue sky.

tracks.title: Peak Hour
tracks.lore: The last track Kayan East finished before sunrise — he called the drop 'interrupting the timeline' in full Yeezus-red confidence and refused to explain further.

tracks.title: Full Send
tracks.lore: A deliberate excess of everything that worked on Neon District — Kayan East calls it the receipt; the dancefloor calls it mandatory eastbound peak hour.

tracks.title: Afterglow
tracks.lore: The cool-down lap on 1989 Drift — Taylor Drift wrote it for when the mountain straightens, the Polaroid light goes gold, and the blonde behind the wheel finally exhales.

tracks.title: Cooldown
tracks.lore: Inspired by opening a window after cooking — Kayan East kept the shuffle slow, like air finally finding the east-facing room.

tracks.title: Last Light
tracks.lore: Lana Del Delay's closing sketch — one sustained note held until the bus reaches the depot, still echoing after everyone left.

tracks.title: Delay Lines
tracks.lore: Lana Del Delay traced the bus route on a map and assigned each stop a delay time — the pad only resolves when every line finally catches up.

tracks.title: Feedback Room
tracks.lore: Recorded in a rehearsal space where the monitors never quite stopped ringing — Lana Del Delay called the room a collaborator and billed it as co-producer.

tracks.title: Pillow Drop
tracks.lore: Jay-Zzz claims the kick lands on the exact frame his head hits the pillow — listeners dispute whether that's a compliment or a warning.

tracks.title: Snooze Button
tracks.lore: Built from nine alarm-clock samples layered until none of them agreed on the time — Jay-Zzz says that's the whole Blueprint Nap philosophy.

tracks.title: Turnstile Stripes
tracks.lore: Justin Tiger sampled a turnstile in triple time and dressed it in tiger-stripe funk — cardio playlists adopted it before he finished the mix.

tracks.title: Metro Fade
tracks.lore: The last train home, doors half-closed, hooks dissolving into platform reverb — Justin Tiger's gentlest commute sketch on Suit & Stripes.

tracks.title: Sub Corridor
tracks.lore: Kayan East walked an east-facing basement corridor and tuned the sub to the echo between concrete pillars — the bear mascot nods from the dropout-orange wall.

tracks.title: Garage Receipt
tracks.lore: Shuffle so tight it sounds like a receipt printer at peak hour — Kayan East stamped EAST on every bar and called the session closed.

tracks.title: District Lights
tracks.lore: Neon signs reflected in wet pavement became the arpeggio — Kayan East insists the district itself wrote the hook, he just pointed the compass east.

tracks.title: Timeline Bend
tracks.lore: A sequel to Peak Hour with twice the ego — Kayan East said the drop bends time itself, then refused follow-up questions at sunrise.

tracks.title: Compass Fade
tracks.lore: The needle settles on east and stays there — Kayan East's wind-down for when the garage corridor finally goes quiet.

tracks.title: Hairpin Gold
tracks.lore: Taylor Drift nailed a downhill hairpin in golden hour and sampled the tire squeal into the lead — every filter sweep is another degree of lean.

tracks.title: Polaroid Hush
tracks.lore: Shot on expired Polaroid stock after the touge run — soft 1989-blue haze, engine cooling, seagulls settling on the guardrail.

tracks.title: Drift Crown
tracks.lore: Taylor Drift's victory lap — platinum-blonde optimism in the rearview, matte white coupe crowned by pink taillights on the last straight home.

tracks.title: Desk Fog
tracks.lore: Jay-Zzz mixed this so quietly the desk lamp flicker became the sidechain — if you notice the kick, you are awake wrong.

tracks.title: Library Loop
tracks.lore: Sampled from a library HVAC vent and stretched until the due dates stopped mattering — Jay-Zzz calls it reference material.

tracks.title: Pillow Logic
tracks.lore: Built from a pillow impression on a MIDI pad — Jay-Zzz insists the groove improves if you do not lift your head.

tracks.title: Last Platform
tracks.lore: Recorded after the last train left — Justin Tiger kept the platform reverb and deleted every hook louder than a whisper.

tracks.title: Fluorescent Pocket
tracks.lore: Justin Tiger locked a funk pocket to the flicker rate of platform fluorescents — tiger stripes optional, head-nod mandatory.

tracks.title: Tiger Focus
tracks.lore: A deep-focus sketch for overnight ticket inspectors — Justin Tiger says the bass only moves if your attention drifts.

tracks.title: Warm Set
tracks.lore: Dua Lift-a wrote this for the first five minutes on the treadmill — silver disco that refuses to peak before you're warm.

tracks.title: Squat Drop
tracks.lore: The drop lands on the downbeat of every squat — Dua Lift-a timed it with a metronome clipped to a barbell collar.

tracks.title: PR Hour
tracks.lore: Recorded in a mirror-walled gym at closing — Dua Lift-a says the last rep and the last chorus must finish together.

tracks.title: Dancefloor Rep
tracks.lore: Dua Lift-a dragged the squat rack onto the dancefloor for one night — the kick still counts reps even when nobody's lifting.

tracks.title: Mirror Ballast
tracks.lore: A silver disco ball weighted like a dumbbell inspired the low end — Dua Lift-a insists balance training applies to basslines.

tracks.title: One More Set
tracks.lore: The encore nobody asked for and everybody needed — Dua Lift-a loops the chorus until the house lights pretend they're strobes.

tracks.title: Amber Steps
tracks.lore: Black Toffee built this from heel taps on a rooftop stairwell at golden hour — every step lands soft, then the shaker answers from the skyline.

tracks.title: Market Moon
tracks.lore: A night-market groove for the ride home — Black Toffee sampled distant vendors, then tucked the voices into percussion instead of lyrics.

tracks.title: Sunset Calls
tracks.lore: The rooftop workout track — Black Toffee wrote the hook as a call-and-response between the sunset, the shaker, and the last set of the day.

tracks.title: Palm Circuit
tracks.lore: Black Toffee wired palm-leaf shadows into the sequencer — the groove loops like rooftop string lights until the kick finds every corner.

tracks.title: Toffee Signal
tracks.lore: A focus groove sent through a sticky delay chain — Black Toffee kept the drums crisp so the bass could stretch without smearing the workday.

tracks.title: Rooftop Syrup
tracks.lore: The title track turns the whole roof into a chorus — Black Toffee calls it syrup because the bass pours slowly but sticks to everyone.

tracks.title: Sync or Swim
tracks.lore: A dance challenge so intense BlackSync nicknamed it swim practice — sink on the count or sync on the drop.

tracks.title: Boom-Boom Wave
tracks.lore: BlackSync's Sync Hour title track — the boom-boom hook started as a warm-up chant and became the loudest song on the comeback schedule.

tracks.title: Girl Crush Glitch
tracks.lore: A timeline glitch in the edit bay became the signature ear-candy — BlackSync kept it because 'perfect sync is boring.'

tracks.title: Basement Mirror
tracks.lore: After the comeback live, BlackSync recorded the quiet version in the basement practice mirror — same choreography, whisper volume.

tracks.title: Finale Formation
tracks.lore: The closing formation on every BlackSync tour — four silhouettes, one heartbeat, zero room for improvisation.

tracks.title: Afterstage Hush
tracks.lore: Recorded backstage after the encore — lightsticks still glowing, choreography finally off, just the metronome breathing slow.

tracks.title: Denim Dawn
tracks.lore: NuJean wrote this at sunrise through a laundromat window — the first light hits the denim rack and the kick finally wakes up.

tracks.title: Bunny Hop
tracks.lore: The bunny-mascot sticker on NuJean's practice mirror became the bounce pattern — two hops, one skip, every bar.

tracks.title: Get Up Jean
tracks.lore: NuJean's debut title track — written for the first warm-up lap around a roller rink before school, when the jeans still smell like detergent.

tracks.title: Roller Ring
tracks.lore: The roller-rink PA announcement became the hook — NuJean looped 'all skaters clockwise' until it felt like a chorus.

tracks.title: Pocket Stitch
tracks.lore: Recorded while hand-stitching a pocket patch — NuJean kept the needle clicks in the percussion because 'focus should sound handmade.'

tracks.title: Blue Habit
tracks.lore: A party track about wearing the same denim three days in a row — NuJean calls it a habit, their stylist calls it a crisis.

tracks.title: Super Shrink
tracks.lore: NuJean left a demo tape in a jeans pocket through the wash — it came out one BPM faster and twice as catchy. They kept it.

tracks.title: Laundry Day
tracks.lore: The quiet B-side for when the spin cycle ends — NuJean says the best hooks are folded warm, not hung loud.

tracks.title: Attention Ring
tracks.lore: A notification tone sampled into the hook — NuJean built a focus track from the one ring they always meant to answer.

tracks.title: Hive Mind
tracks.lore: Five members, one bounce pattern — NuJean choreographed this in a group chat until every phone vibrated on the same beat.

tracks.title: Daydream Wash
tracks.lore: Recorded through the laundromat window on a rainy afternoon — the hook dissolves like detergent in warm water.

tracks.title: Jean Pool
tracks.lore: After the comeback schedule cleared, NuJean left one tape running in an empty jean pool — just the hum of denim and a metronome on low.

tracks.title: Frame Wake
tracks.lore: Sawa Node wrote this while watching hangar lights cycle from red to green — the strings don't start until the frame stands up.

tracks.title: Blueprint Pulse
tracks.lore: A study-session score built from wireframe hum — Sawa Node says the blueprint grid is the metronome.

tracks.title: Launch Vector
tracks.lore: The gym track that counts down from ten — Sawa Node mapped every rep to a thruster ignition.

tracks.title: Hangar Glow
tracks.lore: After sortie, the hangar stays amber — Sawa Node left the strings humming like idle thrusters.

tracks.title: Cockpit Countdown
tracks.lore: Commute music as pre-flight checklist — every synth stab is another system green.

tracks.title: Thruster Line
tracks.lore: Deep-work fuel with a straight thruster burn — no detours, just brass and propulsion.

tracks.title: Anthem Protocol
tracks.lore: The party track that sounds like a season finale — Sawa Node wrote the chorus as a launch authorization chant.

tracks.title: Siege Choir
tracks.lore: Gym fuel stacked so high the squat rack felt like a siege tower — Sawa Node wrote every choir hit for the last set of the day.

tracks.title: Orbital Drop
tracks.lore: The drop hits at orbital altitude — Sawa Node synced the EDM surge to re-entry heat.

tracks.title: Red Shift
tracks.lore: Sunset commute as atmospheric re-entry — the strings turn red before the city appears.

tracks.title: Final Sortie
tracks.lore: Last set, last sortie — Sawa Node removed every quiet bar until only propulsion remained.

tracks.title: Afterburn Haze
tracks.lore: Post-battle cooldown — thruster heat fades into string haze and hangar echo.

tracks.title: Render Pass
tracks.lore: Zone Render's gym OP — guitars rendered at 120% and the chorus vector-locks on every downbeat.

tracks.title: Wireframe Rush
tracks.lore: Focus mode as wireframe preview — Zone Render kept the guitars thin until the hook solidifies.

tracks.title: Particle Beam
tracks.lore: Peak-hour particle beam — the chorus hits once and leaves a scorch mark on the timeline.

tracks.title: Zero Gravity Verse
tracks.lore: The quiet verse after orbital combat — Zone Render unplugged the distortion and let the frame float.

tracks.title: Cowbell Crest
tracks.lore: GTR-808 mapped every cowbell hit to a hairpin — the crest is the loudest turn on the touge.

tracks.title: Memphis Touge
tracks.lore: Memphis sample chop meets mountain pass — GTR-808 says the guardrail is the hi-hat.

tracks.title: Drift Logic
tracks.lore: Peak-hour drift logic — when the 808 drops, the whole dancefloor slides sideways.

tracks.title: Night 808
tracks.lore: Late-night focus run — purple streetlights and a 808 that never swerves.

tracks.title: Guardrail Grind
tracks.lore: After the run — GTR-808 sampled guardrail scrape and tuned it to a warm 808 hum.

tracks.title: Bassline Slide
tracks.lore: The quietest phonk in the catalog — GTR-808 pulled the cowbell back until only bass slides remained.

tracks.title: Bell Hammer
tracks.lore: Peak-hour warehouse set — Smoke Shift hammers the cowbell until the smoke alarm gives up.

tracks.title: Smoke Stack
tracks.lore: Every set gets a cowbell count-in — Smoke Shift vents the 808 through a smoke stack between reps.

tracks.title: Warehouse 808
tracks.lore: Late-night warehouse session — Smoke Shift locked the cowbell to a steady eight-count and let the 808 do the thinking.

tracks.title: Shift Lock
tracks.lore: Commute as gear change — Smoke Shift says third to fourth is where the cowbell catches fire.

tracks.title: After Hours Bell
tracks.lore: After the warehouse clears — one cowbell echo and a warm 808 humming in the dark.

tracks.title: Rep Count
tracks.lore: The quietest warehouse phonk — Smoke Shift turned the cowbell into a metronome and the 808 into a desk vibration.

tracks.title: Amen Protocol
tracks.lore: Peak-hour neurofunk — Amen Rush chopped the break so hard the dancefloor forgot gravity exists.

tracks.title: Sub Roll
tracks.lore: Gym PR fuel — Amen Rush mapped every roll to a rep and every sub drop to a plate change.

tracks.title: Break Focus
tracks.lore: Deep-work DnB — Amen Rush kept the break steady and the reese bass just wobbly enough to stay awake.

tracks.title: Rollout
tracks.lore: Morning commute at breakneck speed — Amen Rush says the train is late but the amen break isn't.

tracks.title: Liquid Rush
tracks.lore: Liquid cooldown after the neuro set — Amen Rush softened the break until it flowed like warm sub bass.

tracks.title: Neuro Fade
tracks.lore: Catalog track one hundred — Amen Rush stripped the neuro stabs until only a heartbeat break and a whisper of reese remained.
